event: Cincinnatus receiving the ambassadors from Rome
symbols: plow and oxen — honest labor and humility before being called to power
toga of the ambassadors — authority of the Roman state summoning the farmer
bare-chested Cincinnatus — simplicity and readiness for service, a man unattached to status
gesture of address — the moment of call, echoing biblical vocation narratives
distant landscape with city — Rome awaiting its deliverer
description: A bare-chested man in a simple white loincloth stands at left beside a pair of yoked oxen and a plow, one hand raised in a gesture of address or surprise. Before him stand several richly dressed men in Roman togas of red, orange, and white, the foremost figures gesturing formally toward him. Additional figures with horses gather behind the ambassadors. The setting is a rural landscape with rolling hills, scattered trees, and a distant city on a rocky promontory under a partly cloudy sky. The composition contrasts the humble farmer with the grandeur of the Roman delegation.

_meta.title: Cincinnatus Receiving The Ambassadors From Rome
